messages.text:
cycle108_official_source_adapter_9pro_packet.zip
Zip Archive
You are one of nine independent theorem workers attacking the RS-MCA / Proximity

Prize M1 upper-side route. You have a context packet with the current canonical

tracker, Cycle106 and Cycle107 audits, selected Cycle107 role returns, source

extracts, q-ledger material, and auxiliary F1/L1/L2/X1 notes.


No Internet. Use only the attached context and your own reasoning.


Try to fully solve the problem. If you cannot fully solve it, progress it as

much as possible. Take all the time you need. Use MAX reasoning. Do not give a

polished vague essay. Give a theorem, counterexample mechanism, exact wall, or

checker/certificate target.


The active target is:


L-CYCLE108-OFFICIAL-RESIDUAL-SLOPE-PARTITION-INJECTIVE-NORMALIZATION-AND-AP-DESCENT


Prove or refute:


Every official residual bad slope in the M1 upper-side source problem is either

charged to a banked quotient/periodic/contained/tangent/endpoint/field-ledger

branch, or assigned injectively to a polynomial-size tagged normalization chart

whose activity is an endpoint-corrected complement-line or equivalent

AP-corrected Gate B object. The number of tags/charts must be polynomial in n,

with exponent independent of s and k. The official corrected-reserve

aperiodicity predicate must descend to the AP_corr or rank-escape hypothesis

consumed by Gate B.


You must preserve the exact claim labels:


PROOF

COUNTERPACKET

BANKABLE_LEMMA

ROUTE_CUT

EXACT_NEW_WALL

AUDIT

PLAN


Do you see a route to a full solve? If yes, what is the next exact lemma or

construction? If no, identify the first exact reduction line where the theorem

fails and give the precise counterexample mechanism.


Do not claim prize-level progress unless you have the full official source

adapter, q-field transfer, and numerator bound. Finite/model certificates are

not prize proofs. A model counterpacket is not a source-valid counterpacket

unless it is embedded in the official above-reserve source problem and all

charges are paid.


You are one of nine independent theorem workers attacking the RS-MCA / Proximity


Prize M1 upper-side route. You have a context packet with the current canonical


tracker, Cycle106 and Cycle107 audits, selected Cycle107 role returns, source


extracts, q-ledger material, and auxiliary F1/L1/L2/X1 notes.


No Internet. Use only the attached context and your own reasoning.


Try to fully solve the problem. If you cannot fully solve it, progress it as


much as possible. Take all the time you need. Use MAX reasoning. Do not give a


polished vague essay. Give a theorem, counterexample mechanism, exact wall, or


checker/certificate target.


The active target is:


L-CYCLE108-OFFICIAL-RESIDUAL-SLOPE-PARTITION-INJECTIVE-NORMALIZATION-AND-AP-DESCENT


Prove or refute:


Every official residual bad slope in the M1 upper-side source problem is either


charged to a banked quotient/periodic/contained/tangent/endpoint/field-ledger


branch, or assigned injectively to a polynomial-size tagged normalization chart


whose activity is an endpoint-corrected complement-line or equivalent


AP-corrected Gate B object. The number of tags/charts must be polynomial in n,


with exponent independent of s and k. The official corrected-reserve


aperiodicity predicate must descend to the AP_corr or rank-escape hypothesis


consumed by Gate B.


You must preserve the exact claim labels:


PROOF


COUNTERPACKET


BANKABLE_LEMMA


ROUTE_CUT


EXACT_NEW_WALL


AUDIT


PLAN


Do you see a route to a full solve? If yes, what is the next exact lemma or


construction? If no, identify the first exact reduction line where the theorem


fails and give the precise counterexample mechanism.


Do not claim prize-level progress unless you have the full official source


adapter, q-field transfer, and numerator bound. Finite/model certificates are


not prize proofs. A model counterpacket is not a source-valid counterpacket


unless it is embedded in the official above-reserve source problem and all


charges are paid.

Before finalizing, do a self-audit.


Explicitly answer:


1. What exact implication did I prove, and what exact implication did I not prove?

2. Is the result official-prize-relevant, or only a finite/model/research certificate?

3. What is the first line in the reduction chain where the theorem could fail?

4. Are q_gen, q_line, q_code, q_chal, and the 2^-128 target being used correctly?

5. Could quotient/periodic structure, contained incidences, same-slope collisions,

   tangent supports, endpoint terms, high-denominator branches, or affine color

   normalization reduce the claimed numerator?

6. If my answer is a PLAN, what exact theorem/checker/counterpacket would

   convert it into PROOF or COUNTERPACKET?


Do not hide behind broad language. Give the exact next lemma, exact missing

hypothesis, or exact counterexample mechanism. # Role 08 - Q-Ledger And Numerator Transfer

Audit the official numerator transfer.

Assume the best possible Cycle108 source adapter and Gate B theorem. Determine
exactly what this implies for:

q_gen
q_line
q_code
q_chal
2^-128 target
same-slope collisions
field-extension branches
affine color normalization
tangent support floors

Prove the final official numerator inequality if the preceding hypotheses are
sufficient. If they are not sufficient, identify the missing field-ledger or
normalization hypothesis precisely.

This role should prevent false prize-level claims from model-level or wrong-field
proofs.
Show more

messages.text: IтАЩll reconstruct the Cycle108 reduction and q-ledger, then test numerator transfer under the strongest adapter/Gate B assumptions while isolating source-valid implications from model-only gaps.

messages.className: min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&]:mt-1
messages.text: The tagged-cover/Gate B count appears to control distinct slopes, not witnesses, and the first serious gap is field transferтАФespecially the undefined q_code alias versus the canonical q-ledger.

messages.className: min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&]:mt-1
messages.text: The conditional transfer is exact only up to two closures: extension-valued lines must be covered in the actual line field, and the polynomial numerator needs an explicit integer bound for 128-bit soundness.

COUNTERPACKET

No source-valid corrected-reserve COUNTERPACKET follows from this audit.

The finite F1 examples refute unrestricted native-to-extension transfer, but they are not embedded here into the official above-reserve source problem with all quotient, periodic, endpoint, tangent, contained, and field charges paid.

A valid q-ledger counterpacket would have to exhibit an official corrected-reserve K

AUDIT

Exact implication proved.
I proved:

field-faithful disjoint adapter+explicit branch caps+injective tagged charts+absolute Gate B eliminantsтЗТM

I did not prove the official source partition, AP descent, Gate B escape, extension-valued balanced residue-cloud bound, tangent upper charge, or the numerical inequality (17) for any prize parameter set.

Status.
This is official-prize-relevant as a theorem-level numerator-transfer lemma. It is not a prize proof and not merely a finite/model certificate. Its application remains conditional on missing official source theorems.

First possible failure.
In the current overall chain, the first failure remains the official residual-slope partition/injective normalization. Under the roleтАЩs assumed best adapter and Gate B, the first q-specific failure is (16), the transfer from the native code field to genuinely extension-valued lines. If that transfer is included, the first remaining failure is the explicit budget (17).

Field use.

q
gen
	тАЛ

: corrected reserve, entropy, and source-aperiodicity premises only.

q
code
	тАЛ

: native code alphabet and source parity-check typing.

q
line
	тАЛ

: actual bad-slope field and native MCA denominator.

q
chal
	тАЛ

: protocol sampling denominator only through the fiber formula (13).

2
тИТ128
: used only after the complete official numerator has been assembled.

Possible numerator reductions.

Same-slope support collisions: deduplicate completely.

Literal whole-coset swaps forcing the same slope: zero additional numerator.

Nontrivial periodic orbits and hidden quotient-action-rank components: genuinely distinct slopes; must be paid.

Contained-only incidences: zero; a slope with any transverse witness still counts once.

Tangent supports: do not reduce the numerator; they impose the floor (18).

Endpoint: at most one in the banked official endpoint regime.

High-denominator and balanced arbitrary-anchor branches: no automatic reduction; they need explicit source caps or tagged charts.

Global nonzero affine normalization: exact cardinality preservation.

Chart-dependent affine normalization: valid only with retained tags.

Base-valued lines in an extension: exact subfield confinement via (11).

Genuinely extension-valued lines: no native same-numerator transfer.
